title: Human Bond Dynamics Signal Definitions
purpose: Provides operational definitions for Human Bond Dynamics signals so that relational narratives can be evaluated consistently and explained transparently.

signals.name: Rapid Emotional Convergence
signals.description: Detected when two individuals form a strong emotional bond within an unusually short timeframe.
inclusion_criteria: Intense attachment language appears within days or very few encounters
Immediate sense of exceptional recognition or destiny
exclusion_criteria: Ordinary attraction without strong attachment language
signals.false_positive_risk: Strong first attraction may resemble convergence without creating a durable bond structure.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when combined with dependency escalation or separation anxiety.

signals.name: Mutual Vulnerability Disclosure
signals.description: Detected when both parties disclose emotionally significant history early in the bond.
inclusion_criteria: Reciprocal disclosure of divorce, grief, loneliness, or fear
signals.false_positive_risk: Open conversation can be mistaken for deep vulnerability when emotional stakes remain low.
signals.escalation_threshold: Becomes structurally important when paired with rapid convergence or dependency escalation.

signals.name: Boundary Shock Event
signals.description: Detected when an unexpected behavior or signal abruptly destabilizes trust, certainty, or emotional temperature.
inclusion_criteria: Sudden freezing after a perceived boundary crossing
exclusion_criteria: Minor misunderstanding with no relational destabilization
signals.false_positive_risk: A brief surprise may look like shock even when the bond structure remains stable.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when followed by withdrawal, rupture, or repeated mistrust.

signals.name: Freeze Response
signals.description: Detected when one party emotionally shuts down, withdraws, or becomes inaccessible under relational stress.
exclusion_criteria: Short pause used for self-regulation without detachment
signals.false_positive_risk: Healthy boundary-taking may resemble freezing when context is thin.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when repeated as a dominant rupture pattern.

signals.name: Reconciliation Pullback
signals.description: Detected when a rupture is followed by an active attempt to restore connection, reassurance, or closeness.
inclusion_criteria: Direct attempt to repair after rupture
Mutual return after freeze or conflict
signals.false_positive_risk: Short-term reunion may be mistaken for repair when the underlying instability remains unchanged.
signals.escalation_threshold: Becomes structurally important when repeated in a cycle with freeze and rupture.

signals.name: Dependency Escalation
signals.description: Detected when emotional reliance increases rapidly and the bond becomes central to stability, meaning, or daily regulation.
inclusion_criteria: Emotional functioning tied tightly to the other person
signals.false_positive_risk: Intense early love can look dependent without true autonomy loss.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when combined with separation anxiety, jealousy, or repeated rupture cycles.

signals.name: Separation Anxiety Spike
signals.description: Detected when physical distance or temporary loss of contact produces disproportionate distress.
inclusion_criteria: Marked anguish before or during separation
Extreme distress around travel or distance
Strong collapse in emotional state when contact is threatened
signals.false_positive_risk: Romantic longing may resemble anxiety without becoming structurally destabilizing.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when the relationship cannot maintain equilibrium under ordinary separation.

signals.name: Attachment Loop Cycle
signals.description: Detected when the bond repeatedly cycles through connection, rupture, and reconnection.
inclusion_criteria: Instability becomes cyclical rather than exceptional
exclusion_criteria: Single rupture followed by stable repair
signals.false_positive_risk: A short, intense early phase may look cyclical before the bond stabilizes.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when the loop becomes the dominant structure of the relationship.

signals.name: Asymmetric Emotional Trigger
signals.description: Detected when one party experiences intense emotional impact from stimuli the other does not experience as equally significant.
inclusion_criteria: Different symbolic meanings assigned to the same event
signals.false_positive_risk: Ordinary sensitivity differences may look structurally asymmetrical when they are situational.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when it repeatedly destabilizes trust or reconciliation.

signals.name: Jealousy Lockdown Pattern
signals.description: Detected when jealousy causes emotional closure, withdrawal, rigidity, or repeated relational shutdown.
inclusion_criteria: Silence or shutdown after jealousy trigger
Rigid emotional closure following perceived threat
signals.false_positive_risk: Ordinary insecurity may look like lockdown if only one episode is visible.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates when jealousy becomes a recurrent control or shutdown mechanism.
